title: Glyciphylla hispidula
taxonomy: Glyciphylla hispidula is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Goodeniaceae. It belongs to the genus Glyciphylla, which is a group of plants native to Australia. The plant is commonly known as the Leptospermum-like Goodenia or the Leptospermum-like Goodenia. Glyciphylla hispidula is a member of the order Asterales and the clade angiosperms. This species is endemic to Australia, specifically in the southeastern part of the country.
uses: Glyciphylla hispidula, also known as the Rose Mallee, has been used in traditional Australian medicine for various purposes. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat wounds and skin conditions.
